name: Large commercial quote with multiple sections
description: $58,400 commercial roof project with materials, labor, equipment rental, and permits. Stress test for layout — the action card and downstream public quote page must remain readable, not overflow.

persona.name: Ahmed Salah
persona.context: Bidding on a small commercial flat-roof job. Higher stakes — wants the quote to look as professional as a print PDF.

steps.action: Types a long multi-line description with 7 line items: TPO membrane $18,200, insulation $9,400, fasteners/adhesive $2,800, labor 6 days $19,800, crane rental $3,400, dumpster $1,200, permits $3,600
steps.expect: Assistant produces a single create_quote with all 7 line items and a coherent summary
Line items wrap cleanly; long descriptions don't overflow the card; total $58,400.00 is readable

steps.action: Sends the quote and opens the public-quote URL in a new tab
steps.expect: Customer-facing page renders with the same line items, totals, and a clear Accept button

evaluationCriteria.check: Currency columns align across all rows; no jagged right edges
Big totals don't feel cramped — typography handles 5- and 6-figure amounts gracefully
Public quote URL renders without auth and is shareable; opening it counts as an open exactly once
Public quote page passes basic axe checks: contrast, single h1, labelled form controls
